definition_summary: Human made and not natural; may also mean imitation or forced.
key_distinctions: Carries multiple senses across made object substitute and affectation domains

definition_summary: Not real genuine or true; counterfeit or sham.
key_distinctions: Deception and non genuineness are central rather than incidental
contrasts_with_artificial: neutral human made branch; regulatory additive branch

definition_summary: Not true; deceptive; not genuine; sometimes a substitute.
key_distinctions: Truth error and misleading senses are broader than artificial
notes: Can touch both statement and object domains.

definition_summary: Imitation made with intent to deceive.
key_distinctions: Fraud intent is stronger and narrower than artificial
contrasts_with_artificial: constructed neutral branch; synthetic material branch

definition_summary: Pretended false or counterfeit; a fraud or false show.
key_distinctions: Performance and pretence dominate more than manufacture
contrasts_with_artificial: artificial satellite and other neutral built uses

definition_summary: Chemically formed by human agency rather than natural origin; by extension false or pretended.
key_distinctions: Material and chemical process are more central than in artificial
overlaps_with_artificial: material additive and non natural branch
contrasts_with_artificial: sincerity branch; many neutral built but non chemical uses

definition_summary: Copy or counterfeit; designed to imitate a genuine article.
contrasts_with_artificial: broad made branch that does not aim to copy
notes: Closer in object substitution than in origin.

definition_summary: Made to look genuine or used to model a system or event.
key_distinctions: Representation and modeling are more central than manufacture
notes: Needs more corpus for technical uses.

definition_summary: Convincingly rendered to appear natural or real.
key_distinctions: Representation effect rather than ontological status
notes: Must not be collapsed into real.

definition_summary: Actual or genuine; not counterfeit artificial or imitation.
key_distinctions: Ontological and authenticity claim stronger than realistic
notes: Strongest contrast anchor with natural in some contexts.

definition_summary: Possessing the claimed origin or quality; sincere; not counterfeit.
key_distinctions: Adds sincerity and provenance more explicitly than real
overlaps_with_artificial: opposes artificial manners and not genuine sense
contrasts_with_artificial: artificial when used for feigned or substitute objects

definition_summary: Not false or imitation; real; trustworthy; true to an original.
overlaps_with_artificial: opposes artificial at the not genuine edge
contrasts_with_artificial: neutral human made branch and not natural branch
notes: Close to genuine but more certification coded.

definition_summary: Existing in or formed by nature and not by humans; also spontaneous and minimally processed.
overlaps_with_artificial: opposes artificial across material and manner senses
contrasts_with_artificial: artificial built processed or contrived uses

key_distinctions: Neutral assembly sense without necessary deception

definition_summary: Produced by hand or machinery at scale; sometimes made up figuratively.
Subjected to a special treatment or manufacturing process.
key_distinctions: Treatment and handling are central rather than imitation
contrasts_with_artificial: real or fake binary by itself
